prompt: Single-frame copperplate scientific engraving aesthetic from the Age of Exploration (16th-18th century), monochrome black ink on warm ivory paper, dense cross-hatching, stippling, engraved contour lines, precise linework, ornamental but period-appropriate border cartouche with no words. Depict a physical mountainous terrain as if documented by a natural philosopher: many smooth ridges and valleys converging toward one deepest basin at the center. Through the terrain, show one continuous descending footpath traced by small realistic bootprints and occasional walking-stick marks that progressively move from steep high ridges into gentler slopes and finally into the central basin. Include several plausible alternate side paths that stall in shallow depressions and do not reach the deepest basin, to imply local minima. Add period field instruments lying on rocks (compass, brass divider, small satchel), rendered as physical objects only. No modern objects, no equations, no text, no numbers, no labels, no arrows, no graph axes, no neon effects. Composition must make the concept readable to an ML expert: iterative downhill steps guided by slope, toward a minimum, with risk of getting trapped in local minima. Keep everything physically plausible, as if an 18th-century expedition plate observed in nature. High detail, museum-print quality. Negative prompt: modern infographic, chart, line graph, scatter plot, glossy 3D render, photoreal camera blur, digital UI overlay, typography, captions, watermark, split panel.
tradition_name: Scientific engraving from the Age of Exploration
tradition_approach: Used copperplate cross-hatching, monochrome ink on ivory paper, contour-driven terrain, and expedition-era instrument motifs so iterative downhill movement reads as gradient descent within authentic engraving grammar.
